summary.strongest_product_qualification: ELF is the strongest measured integrated product in the checked-in Agent Knowledge OS matrix because all six ELF-owned layers now have source-linked readback, tests, and docs evidence. This is not a broad claim that ELF beats every competitor on every competitor-owned specialty.
summary.coverage_statement: Only ELF has complete same-repo coverage across all six Agent Knowledge OS layers. qmd, agentmemory, OpenViking, mem0/OpenMemory, claude-mem, memsearch, Letta, graph/RAG projects, llm-wiki, gbrain, PageIndex, and OpenKB have partial, blocked, or reference-only coverage depending on the scenario.

supported_scenarios.completion_rule: Captured sources remain documents with source refs, hydration, search, and bounded replay; they are not silently converted into authoritative memory notes.
Current, historical, tombstoned, stale, superseded, selected, and dropped evidence stays explicit under read-profile policy.
Knowledge pages are rebuildable derived artifacts with source refs, lint, version diffs, and no hidden source-of-truth mutation.
Graph facts are typed Postgres readback over sourced, inferred, ambiguous, stale, and superseded markers, not a second graph database authority.
Background proposals stay reviewable with source refs, affected refs, lint, diff, policy, and review audit before high-impact mutation.
Selected, dropped, available, reviewable, not_requested, and blocked rows remain visible across memory, docs, pages, graph facts, and proposals.

product_matrix.strongest_advantage: Policy-gated, source-linked, replayable memory and knowledge authority across all six layers.
product_matrix.remaining_gap: Needs a richer operator UI and more same-corpus adapters for competitor specialties.
product_matrix.evidence: README checked-in live benchmark snapshot plus June 20 component reports.

product_matrix.strongest_advantage: Local retrieval pipeline, query expansion, weighted fusion, rerank, short CLI replay, and top-k debug ergonomics.
product_matrix.remaining_gap: Does not expose ELF-style source-authority, dropped-candidate, service trace, page rebuild, graph-lite, or review queue semantics in the current adapter.

product_matrix.strongest_advantage: Cross-agent hooks, MCP/REST packaging, local viewer, and coding-agent continuity workflow references.
product_matrix.remaining_gap: Durable storage, lifecycle/cold-start proof, and capture breadth remain incomplete or blocked in the checked-in evidence.

product_matrix.strongest_advantage: Filesystem-like context URIs, hierarchy, staged trajectory, and recursive context expansion as a product idea.
product_matrix.remaining_gap: Same-corpus staged trajectory, hierarchy selection, and recursive expansion stay blocked until evidence-bearing stage artifacts exist.

product_matrix.strongest_advantage: Entity-scoped memory history, hosted ecosystem, OpenMemory UI/export direction, and optional graph memory concept.
product_matrix.remaining_gap: OpenMemory product UI/export remains blocked locally; hosted Platform and optional graph memory are non-goals for this lane.

product_matrix.strongest_advantage: Progressive disclosure UX, local inspection, and automatic capture-loop reference behavior.
product_matrix.remaining_gap: Hook/viewer capture proof is still blocked in Docker-contained checked-in evidence.

product_matrix.strongest_advantage: Markdown-first canonical store, incremental reindex, and practical hybrid retrieval workflow.
product_matrix.remaining_gap: Broader prompt behavior, TTL, knowledge-page rebuild, graph, and review queue coverage are not encoded.

product_matrix.strongest_advantage: Core versus archival memory model and export/readback product concept.
product_matrix.remaining_gap: Contained Letta export/readback remains disabled by default and blocked until core block JSON, archival readback/search JSON, and source ids exist.

product_matrix.strongest_advantage: Temporal graph validity vocabulary and graph-memory product reference.
product_matrix.remaining_gap: Representative graph/RAG slice remains blocked by provider/graph-store setup and lacks comparable same-corpus fact artifacts.

product_matrix.strongest_advantage: Graph-oriented retrieval and citation/navigation reference direction.
product_matrix.remaining_gap: No contained same-corpus page/fact artifact comparable to ELF knowledge and graph reports exists yet.

product_matrix.strongest_advantage: RAG workflow and document processing product reference.
product_matrix.remaining_gap: Representative graph/RAG comparison remains blocked until contained adapter outputs exist.
Representative comparison is incomplete, so no parity or loss claim is allowed.

product_matrix.strongest_advantage: Tiny scored graph/RAG smoke target that can expose artifact-shape mismatches.
product_matrix.remaining_gap: Current scored smoke is wrong_result; it is not graph/RAG parity evidence.

product_matrix.strongest_advantage: Compiled wiki and knowledge-page workflow reference.
product_matrix.remaining_gap: No comparable same-corpus scored page artifacts are checked in.

product_matrix.strongest_advantage: Personal knowledge-base and query-save/lint loop reference.
product_matrix.remaining_gap: No comparable scored adapter output is checked in.

product_matrix.strongest_advantage: Agent graph orchestration reference for stateful workflows.
product_matrix.remaining_gap: No comparable Agent Knowledge OS memory/page/graph artifact is checked in for this matrix.

product_matrix.strongest_advantage: Typed relation and small-graph memory reference.
product_matrix.remaining_gap: No comparable same-corpus typed fact artifact is checked in.

product_matrix.strongest_advantage: Vectorless, reasoning-based long-document tree retrieval and PageIndex MCP ecosystem direction.
product_matrix.remaining_gap: No same-corpus PageIndex adapter or long-PDF tree artifact is checked into ELF yet.

product_matrix.strongest_advantage: Document-to-wiki compilation, concept/entity pages, cross-links, lint reports, watch/recompile workflow, and PageIndex-backed long PDF handling.
product_matrix.remaining_gap: OpenKB is a direct knowledge-library reference, but no same-corpus ELF benchmark adapter has run it yet.

competitor_strengths.strength: Transparent local retrieval pipeline and compact replay ergonomics.
competitor_strengths.elf_response: Add user-facing retrieval/fusion/rerank knobs and short replay artifacts on top of the recall debug panel.

competitor_strengths.strength: Long-document tree search without a vector database and PageIndex MCP ecosystem.
competitor_strengths.elf_response: Add a long-document tree adapter path for Source Library and compare it against ELF source refs and page rebuilds.

competitor_strengths.strength: Compiled Markdown wiki, concept/entity pages, saved explorations, lint, watch, and recompile workflows.
competitor_strengths.elf_response: Fold OpenKB-style library management into Knowledge Workspace without weakening ELF source-of-truth boundaries.

competitor_strengths.strength: Staged context trajectory, hierarchy selection, and recursive expansion.
competitor_strengths.elf_response: Emit comparable stage artifacts from ELF recall planning and source-library hydration.

competitor_strengths.strength: Entity-scoped memory history, hosted ecosystem, UI/export direction, and optional graph memory.
competitor_strengths.elf_response: Strengthen history/event APIs, export UX, and optional graph-context channel while preserving policy-gated writes.

competitor_strengths.strength: Core/archive memory split and productized memory export/readback model.
competitor_strengths.elf_response: Keep ELF core/archive source refs, but add contained adapter output before win/tie/loss claims.

competitor_strengths.strength: Temporal graph validity, citation/navigation, and graph retrieval product references.
competitor_strengths.elf_response: Expand graph-lite reports into adapter-backed temporal fact comparison without replacing Postgres authority.

competitor_strengths.strength: Capture hooks, local viewers, and practical continuity UX.
competitor_strengths.elf_response: Build the operator viewer around Source Library, Memory Authority, Dreaming queue, and recall debug surfaces.

optimization_queue.delta: VectifyAI PageIndex/OpenKB are reference-only but directly target long-document library management and knowledge compilation.
optimization_queue.next_action: Create a contained PageIndex/OpenKB adapter over benchmark-owned sources and compare tree/wiki artifacts against ELF source refs, knowledge pages, and recall debug rows.

optimization_queue.delta: qmd keeps the measured retrieval-debug ergonomics edge despite ELF's trace/stage visibility wins.
optimization_queue.next_action: Expose retrieval expansion, fusion, rerank, top-k, and compact replay artifacts in ELF recall/debug surfaces.

optimization_queue.delta: ELF now has source, page, graph, proposal, and debug APIs but no unified knowledge-library management surface.
optimization_queue.next_action: Build a library UI that can ingest saved articles/threads, show source docs, derived pages, graph facts, proposal queue, and replayable recall traces.

optimization_queue.delta: OpenViking-style hierarchy and recursive trajectory remain blocked because comparable stage artifacts are missing.
optimization_queue.next_action: Emit same-corpus stage trajectory, hierarchy selection, rejected siblings, and recursive expansion artifacts.

optimization_queue.delta: Letta core/archive comparison remains blocked until export/search/readback source ids exist.
optimization_queue.next_action: Run contained Letta adapter materialization with core block JSON, archival search/readback JSON, and source ids.

optimization_queue.delta: OpenMemory UI/export remains blocked and mem0 history remains a measured reference advantage.
optimization_queue.next_action: Add product-container UI/export readback and strengthen ELF history/export APIs without weakening write policy.

optimization_queue.delta: Graphiti/Zep, GraphRAG, RAGFlow, LightRAG, graphify, LangGraph, and nanograph remain typed non-pass or reference-only.
optimization_queue.next_action: Produce contained same-corpus graph fact/page/citation artifacts while keeping ELF graph-lite as readback over source authority.

optimization_queue.delta: agentmemory and claude-mem still point to practical capture/viewer UX that ELF has not productized.
optimization_queue.next_action: Add a local operator viewer and capture audit flow across Source Library, Memory Authority, and recall debug traces.

optimization_queue.delta: XY-930 proxy/public-corpus evidence cannot prove real private-corpus or provider-backed quality.
optimization_queue.next_action: Run operator-approved private/provider benchmark only when routed private corpus and provider setup exist.
